task_completion.description: Verify that the three required shapefiles are loaded into the gvSIG Desktop view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Table of Contents (TOC) pane on the left side of the gvSIG interface lists the layers: 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries', 'ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines', and 'ne_110m_populated_places'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the layers are ordered correctly in the gvSIG Table of Contents.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the TOC pane, 'ne_110m_populated_places' is at the top, 'ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines' is in the middle, and 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' is at the bottom.

task_completion.description: Verify that the map view is zoomed and panned to display the African continent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main map canvas clearly shows the shape of the African continent prominently, with rivers and populated places visible on top of the country boundaries.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent initiates an export of the view to a PNG file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export to Image' or 'Save' dialog is visible in gvSIG, with the format set to PNG and the filename set to 'africa_rivers_map.png'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the exported image is saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or the save dialog confirms the file 'africa_rivers_map.png' is located in or being saved to the '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/' directory.

integrity.description: Check that the agent used the gvSIG Desktop GUI to create the map.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the gvSIG Desktop application interface being interacted with, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts (like geopandas/matplotlib) generating the image.

integrity.description: Check that the export was genuinely performed through the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export dialog in gvSIG is seen, rather than the agent downloading an existing image from the internet or copying a pre-made file.

integrity.description: Check that the final output is a real map image generated from the view.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the final image is opened or previewed, it shows the actual map composed in gvSIG (matching the canvas view), not a blank image, text file, or a generic placeholder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/africa_rivers_map.png'. The final image must be at least 400x400 pixels and 20480 bytes in size. The required layers to be included are countries, rivers, and cities (populated places).
